title: PR review loop: detect and fix broken CI
description: The pr-review-loop skill currently ignores CI failures. After commit-and-push, it triggers the next review without checking CI. Need: (1) check-ci.sh script to wait for CI and report failures, (2) SKILL.md update to add CI fix loop between push and next review trigger.
close_reason: Added check-ci.sh script and updated SKILL.md with CI failure handling loop

title: Phase 1.7: motion-bisection for animated sequences
description:
The Phase 1.6 e2e against MIqpvpNS5pI revealed a gap: per-second pHash run-grouping merges sequences where only a SMALL OBJECT moves across an otherwise-static background (e.g. a boat moving across a long landscape with river+hills+sky). The global pHash stays within threshold because most pixels don't change, so the run-grouping treats it as one stable shot and emits only the start frame.

Concrete miss: t=358.792 captured the boat at the LEFT edge of a wide river scene, but the boat's animation across the river over the next ~20 seconds was lost. The user's note: 'we should bisect more when we see a lot of motion happening.'

Implementation options (pick one or combine):

1. Post-classification motion bisection: after a frame is classified as a keep-kind, automatically extract t±2, t±5, t±10 (or until next keep-kind) and look for distinctly-different keep-kind frames in those samples. Add survivors to the keep-list. Cheapest option, fits cleanly in synthesizer Phase A.5.
2. Grid-pHash in screenshotter: split each thumbnail into a 4x4 grid and compute a pHash per cell. A small object moving across a static background changes one or two cells without affecting the global pHash. Run-grouping then merges only when ALL cells are similar to the run's start. More compute, better motion sensitivity.
3. ffmpeg motion-vector primitive: use mestimate or a lower-threshold scene-detect (~0.05) to flag motion-rich regions and emit additional candidates within them.

Acceptance: re-run against MIqpvpNS5pI; the boat-on-river section captures multiple boat positions instead of one edge frame.
notes:
External design input (from a separate Claude conversation, 2026-04-29):

DETECTION SIGNAL — sustained directional optical flow distinguishes progressive animation cleanly:
- Static frame: zero flow
- Static reveal (text/diagram appears in place): zero flow, pHash drifts
- Progressive animation: sustained flow vectors with consistent direction across many frames
This is the third case our current primitives can't catch. Once a span is flagged, dense-resample (0.1-0.25s interval) across the run boundaries we already have from pHash grouping — cost is nothing.

SELECTION WITHIN WINDOW — pick the frame where the moving subject is fully visible and centered, computable from the Farneback flow field with no model:

```python
mag = np.linalg.norm(flow, axis=2)
mask = mag > flow_threshold  # binary: moving pixels

# largest connected component = the subject
nlabels, labels, stats, centroids = cv2.connectedComponentsWithStats(mask.astype(np.uint8))
if nlabels < 2:
    score = 0
else:
    largest = 1 + np.argmax(stats[1:, cv2.CC_STAT_AREA])
    cx, cy = centroids[largest]
    x, y, w, h, area = stats[largest]
    h_frame, w_frame = mask.shape
    touches_edge = x == 0 or y == 0 or x+w >= w_frame or y+h >= h_frame
    center_dist = np.hypot(cx - w_frame/2, cy - h_frame/2) / np.hypot(w_frame/2, h_frame/2)
    score = area * (1 - center_dist) * (0.2 if touches_edge else 1.0)
```

Pick frame in dense resample with max score. Boat-at-edge scores low (touches boundary), boat-in-center scores high (interior + centered).

REFINEMENTS:
1. Connected-components assumes one moving subject — breaks for "lord on horse riding past peasants tending crops." Score combined motion-mask bbox or sum top-K.
2. Binary edge-touch penalty is crude — a close-up cart filling 80% of frame always touches edges and gets unfairly docked. Soften: penalize proportional to fraction of component bbox perimeter coinciding with frame perimeter.

THE BIGGER POINT — `--allow-no-screenshot` mode:
For some progressive animations the prose carries the meaning ("goods moved by water") and the visual is decoration. If max score in a motion cluster falls below threshold, emit a markdown annotation instead of a screenshot:
> [animation: cart and boat moving across landscape, 0:34-0:48]

This produces better notes than always finding some frame to grab. Fits the value-filter principle already in Phase A.

This unifies options 1+3 from the existing description with a deterministic selection rule and an opt-out. Recommend implementing as: (a) optical-flow primitive in screenshotter, (b) synthesizer Phase A motion-bisection step that calls it, (c) Phase D composer learns the markdown-annotation fallback.

title: Phase 1.6 e2e re-validation against MIqpvpNS5pI
description:
Re-run the synthesizer end-to-end against https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MIqpvpNS5pI after rp9, 8h5, b7y land. Compare against Phase 1.5 baseline (PR #12 output, 15 images).

Expected outcomes:
- The TOLL chokepoint diagram (around t=222, 8s static) is captured as an informational image. (Phase 1.5 missed it entirely.)
- The surplus wheat field diagram (around t=282, 5s static) is captured. (Phase 1.5 missed.)
- The detailed terrain map at t=642-659 (18s) is captured. (Phase 1.5 missed.)
- The lord-with-castle frame at t=258 is captured. (Phase 1.5 missed; ffmpeg scene-detect catches it.)
- The ADDS VALUE animated build-up has multiple frames captured, not just the terminal state at t=540. Build-up detection in synthesizer A.5 should re-extract intermediate seconds and keep the grass→sheep, grass→sheep+wool, etc. progression.
- image-08 (boat on river @ t=360) — agent's call. User flagged as bad in Phase 1.5; if the new classification + transcript context still keeps it, fine. If the agent now drops it as redundant with image-07's caloric-battery framing, also fine.
- image-13 (full world map @ t=660) — DROPPED via map-overview kind.
- image-14 (zoomed map @ t=690) — DROPPED via map-overview kind.
- image-15 (region with red dot @ t=810) — KEPT via map-with-overlay kind.
- Cross-time pHash dedup catches any duplicate maps reappearing in later chapters.
- transcript_corrections still logs 'kills' -> 'scales' at t=407.
- Total informational images: probably 18-25 (more than Phase 1.5's 15, but all load-bearing).

Save findings to bd memories afterward.

title: synthesizer Phase A.4: refine map kind classification (overview vs with-overlay)
description:
The Phase 1.5 e2e kept three 'map' frames the user flagged as worthless: image-13 (full world map @ t=660, 18s static), image-14 (zoomed map @ t=690, 25s static), and to a lesser extent image-01/02 (opening world map). The current 'map' kind is too coarse — every map gets kept regardless of whether it adds info beyond 'this is the world.'

Update the kind taxonomy in synthesizer SKILL.md:
- Split 'map' into:
  - 'map-overview': establishing shot of geography with no annotations, callouts, or speaker-relevant markers. DROP unless the surrounding transcript prose specifically discusses 'this region' or 'this part of the map.'
  - 'map-with-overlay': map plus drawn-in markers (red dots, dashed lines, labels, route arrows, radius circles, etc.). KEEP — these are informational.
- Keep 'map' as a fallback when the agent can't decide (defaults to keep, conservative bias).

Test target: the chapter-3 region map at t=807-824 (image-15 in Phase 1.5) IS map-with-overlay (red dot marker on a region) — keep. The chapter-2 world map at t=660 is map-overview — drop unless it's the very first map shown (intro context).

Acceptance criteria for Phase 1.6 e2e:
- image-13 (t=660 world map) — dropped
- image-14 (t=690 zoomed map) — dropped (no overlays, no chapter-specific context)
- image-15 (t=810 region with red dot) — kept (overlay)
- image-01 (t=0 opening world map) — kept as scene-setting establisher
close_reason: Synthesizer SKILL.md Phase A rewritten to consume discover.py from screenshotter (rp9). Old A.2-A.5 (sparse sample + bisect on transcript hints) replaced with A.2 (call discover.py) + A.3 (extract candidates at full res) + A.5 (build-up sequence detection via similar-but-distinct pHashes on adjacent keepers) + A.6 (cross-time pHash dedup, ≤12 hamming, whole-list scan). Frame kinds taxonomy: 'map' split into 'map-overview' (drop unless intro establisher or transcript discusses this region) and 'map-with-overlay' (keep — overlays carry information).

title: synthesizer SKILL.md Phase A: rewrite to consume discover.py output
description:
Replace synthesizer Phase A's current sampling+bisection prose with a flow that consumes screenshotter discover.py.

New Phase A:
A.1 Fetch transcript and metadata (unchanged)
A.2 Call discover.py to get candidate-timestamp list. Manifest contains both phash_runs (sustained content with run_duration) and scene_detect transitions.
A.3 Re-extract candidate frames at full res via existing extract.py — pass in the union timestamp list from the discover.py manifest.
A.4 Classify every frame by kind (taxonomy unchanged: drop talking-head/blank/title-card/pull-quote-card; keep diagram/map/chart/code/ui/b-roll/animated-build-step/hybrid-with-info-overlay).
A.5 Build-up detection for animated sequences: when adjacent keep-kind frames have pHash hamming in 10-25 range and start within ~10s, treat as one build-up sequence and re-extract intermediate seconds (every 2s within the sequence) to capture all build-up steps. Add those to the keep-list.
A.6 Cross-time pHash dedup: walk the entire keep-list (not just adjacent) and drop any frame whose pHash is within Hamming ≤ 12 of an earlier-kept frame. Catches the case where the same world-map appears at chapter 1 opening AND chapter 3 opening (image-01 vs image-13 in Phase 1.5).
A.7 Emit final keep-list of (timestamp, frame_path, kind) triples.

Phase A.5 (old: 'Bisect gaps between consecutive keepers') is dissolved — discover.py's per-second pHash coverage replaces the gap-bisection logic. The 'when transcript suggests visual content' heuristic is no longer needed because we already see every visual moment.

The 'always include t=0.5' rule moves into discover.py's output (it's part of the candidate set).

Coordinate with rp9 (discover.py).
close_reason: Synthesizer SKILL.md Phase A rewritten to consume discover.py from screenshotter (rp9). Old A.2-A.5 (sparse sample + bisect on transcript hints) replaced with A.2 (call discover.py) + A.3 (extract candidates at full res) + A.5 (build-up sequence detection via similar-but-distinct pHashes on adjacent keepers) + A.6 (cross-time pHash dedup, ≤12 hamming, whole-list scan). Frame kinds taxonomy: 'map' split into 'map-overview' (drop unless intro establisher or transcript discusses this region) and 'map-with-overlay' (keep — overlays carry information).

title: screenshotter: add discover.py with ffmpeg scene-detect + per-second pHash run-grouping (hybrid mode)
description:
New script: plugins/youtube-screenshotter/skills/youtube-screenshotter/scripts/discover.py

Purpose: emit a high-recall list of candidate-timestamp triples (timestamp, run_duration, source_signal) for a video, using ffmpeg primitives. Synthesizer Phase A consumes this and decides which to keep via kind classification.

CLI:
  discover.py <URL-or-file> [-o WORK_DIR] [--threshold-scene 0.2] [--threshold-phash 12] [--min-run 3]

Behavior:
1. Resolve <URL-or-file>: if URL, ensure video is downloaded via existing video.py. If file path, use it directly.
2. Run ffmpeg scene-detect at --threshold-scene; capture transition timestamps. Cost: single pass, ~10s for 18min video.
3. Run ffmpeg single-pass per-second thumbnail extraction at low res (-vf 'fps=1,scale=320:180' -q:v 5) into WORK_DIR/discover-thumbs/. Cost: ~30s for 18min video, ~30MB.
4. Compute pHash for each thumbnail (importable function from existing phash.py, or shell out per file).
5. Group consecutive thumbnails into runs where Hamming-distance to run's start frame is ≤ --threshold-phash. (Threshold 12 merges talking-head microexpressions into single runs; threshold 5 was too tight and produced 628 runs of 1-2s each.)
6. Filter runs to duration ≥ --min-run seconds.
7. Output JSON manifest:
   {
     'video_path': '...',
     'duration_seconds': 1119,
     'sources': {
       'scene_detect': [4.8, 6.9, ...],
       'phash_runs': [{'start_t': 82, 'end_t': 86, 'duration': 5, 'phash': '...'}, ...]
     },
     'candidates': [{'timestamp': 82.0, 'kind_hint': 'phash_run', 'run_duration': 5}, {'timestamp': 4.8, 'kind_hint': 'scene_change', 'run_duration': null}, ...]
   }
   The 'candidates' list is the union of scene-detect timestamps and phash-run starts, deduped by proximity (within 1s of an existing entry).

Acceptance criteria (re-run against MIqpvpNS5pI):
- Catches the TOLL diagram start (~t=222) — phash_runs source
- Catches Three-Day Rule diagram (~t=88) — phash_runs source
- Catches lord+castle at t=258 — scene_detect source
- Catches missed terrain map at t=642 (18s run) — phash_runs source
- Total candidates: 60-90 (vs the synthesizer's 42 sparse samples in Phase 1.5)
- All standard library + existing screenshotter scripts; no new Python deps.
close_reason: discover.py implemented; smoke-tested against MIqpvpNS5pI cached video — produces 136 candidates with full coverage of all known-missed targets (TOLL diagram, Three-Day Rule, lord+castle, wheat field, ADDS VALUE early/final, terrain map). Both scene-detect and phash_run sources are working, union+dedup operates as designed.

title: Phase 1.6: image discovery primitives in screenshotter
description:
The Phase 1.5 e2e (PR #12) showed that the synthesizer's sparse-sample-then-bisect approach has poor recall — missed the iconic TOLL chokepoint diagram, the surplus wheat field, multiple ADDS VALUE build-up frames, and a detailed terrain map (run #359, 18s static). The user explicitly wants 'more and more ffmpeg capability over time' added to youtube-screenshotter as the discovery layer.

Goal: replace the synthesizer's manual sampling with a screenshotter-side 'discover.py' tool that uses ffmpeg primitives to produce a high-recall candidate-timestamp list. Synthesizer Phase A becomes: 'call discover.py, re-extract candidates at full res via extract.py, classify by kind.' The screenshotter stays mechanical; the synthesizer stays smart.

Discovery primitives evaluated against MIqpvpNS5pI:
- ffmpeg scene-detect (threshold 0.2): 56 candidates, fast, but MISSES soft-fade diagram starts (Three-Day Rule, TOLL). Catches some short content stretches per-second-pHash filters out (lord+castle at t=258).
- per-second extraction + pHash run-grouping (threshold 12, run ≥3s): comprehensive, single ffmpeg pass with -vf fps=1 -s 320x180 is fast (~30s, ~30MB on disk).
- Hybrid: union of both. ~60-80 candidates per ~18min video. Best recall.
- silencedetect: useless for narration-heavy videos (no silent stretches > 2s in this one).
- freezedetect (n=0.005, d=2): catches every sustained shot including talking-head poses AND diagrams. Useful but doesn't distinguish kinds.

Candidate-timestamp scope only — kind classification stays in the synthesizer.

See bd memories: youtube-synthesizer-phase-1-5-e2e-against-miqpvpns5pi (image count baseline) and youtube-synthesizer-phash-dedup-threshold-5-hamming-is (cross-time dedup gap).

title: synthesizer: add preview.py script for rendering/serving entries
description: Wiki entries use Obsidian wikilink syntax (![[image-NN.png]]) which doesn't render in standard markdown viewers. Add a stdlib-only Python script that converts the markdown to HTML (with wikilink-image rewriting and YAML-frontmatter collapsing) and serves the entry directory over HTTP. Used to eyeball synthesizer output on a headless box.
close_reason: Phase 1.5 e2e revalidation passed: 15 load-bearing images (vs 19 noisy in Phase 1); chapter-level prose with sentence-aware reflow; no mid-sentence cuts; description-driven references (thegrainbound.com + discord.gg) with annotations; soft-correct 'kills'->'scales' applied and logged to transcript_corrections frontmatter; source_description populated; preview.py script added for headless-box rendering. Two SKILL.md tightenings landed (sampling cadence, chapter-boundary snapping). Open follow-ups for the next round are image-quality issues (user request) -- new issues will be filed against the youtube-synthesizer skill, not under t1k.

title: screenshotter: expose video description in metadata
description: Phase 1.5 synthesizer SKILL.md (Phases B and C) requires source_description from the video's yt-dlp info dict to populate frontmatter, drive the hallucination check, and extract description-driven references. Current VideoMetadata dataclass in plugins/youtube-screenshotter/skills/youtube-screenshotter/scripts/video.py is missing the field. Add 'description: str' to VideoMetadata and populate from info.get('description', '') in _parse_metadata. Discovered during t1k.5 e2e against MIqpvpNS5pI.

title: Phase 1.5 e2e re-validation against grain-haul video
description:
Re-run the synthesizer against https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MIqpvpNS5pI ('The Ox That's Breaking Your Fantasy Map', The Grainbound) after the Phase 1.5 SKILL.md rewrite.

Expected outcomes:
- ~6 informational images in the wiki entry, NOT 19 (talking-head, blank, title-cards, pull-quote-cards all dropped)
- The bulk→value wheat-bundle conversion (around scene-09 in Phase 1) shows up as multiple frames if the video animates it as a build-up
- One continuous chapter-level prose flow per chapter; no scene-level headings; no clauses cut mid-sentence
- Chapter titles match the video's exactly: 'The Oxen Paradox: Why Your Map is a Lie', 'Chapter 2', 'Chapter 3', 'Chapter 4 - The WORKFLOW.'
- References section contains the actual URLs from the description: https://thegrainbound.com/resources/ and https://discord.gg/PTnt72aj8B with annotations
- 'kills for measurement' is corrected to 'scales for measurement' with the correction logged in transcript_corrections frontmatter
- Frontmatter has source_description populated with the author's verbatim 'ABOUT THIS VIDEO' prose

Iterate on the SKILL.md if any of these fail; capture tuning insights via 'bd remember' for future videos.

See docs/youtube-wiki-design.md for the full revised spec.
close_reason: Phase 1.5 e2e revalidation passed: 15 load-bearing images (vs 19 noisy in Phase 1); chapter-level prose with sentence-aware reflow; no mid-sentence cuts; description-driven references (thegrainbound.com + discord.gg) with annotations; soft-correct 'kills'->'scales' applied and logged to transcript_corrections frontmatter; source_description populated; preview.py script added for headless-box rendering. Two SKILL.md tightenings landed (sampling cadence, chapter-boundary snapping). Open follow-ups for the next round are image-quality issues (user request) -- new issues will be filed against the youtube-synthesizer skill, not under t1k.

title: synthesizer Phase D: chapter-level prose flow with inline images (option D)
description:
Replace the per-scene heading hierarchy with chapter-level prose flow.

Current behavior (broken):
- ## Scenes / ### chapter / #### Scene N — MM:SS to MM:SS / image / transcript-span structure
- Transcript text per scene = naive concatenation of snippets within the scene's [start_time, end_time) window
- Result: clauses cut mid-sentence at scene boundaries; e.g. '...use the map and work / along. I will not show…'

New behavior:
- One ## section per chapter from chapter_markers
- USE THE AUTHOR'S CHAPTER TITLES VERBATIM — including placeholders like 'Chapter 2', 'Chapter 3'. The chapter structure is the author's deliberate framing. Do NOT synthesize 'better' titles from content.
- Sentence-aware reflow of the full chapter's transcript span: concatenate snippets, break into paragraphs at sentence terminators (. ! ?) at natural feeling points (~3-6 sentences)
- Embed informational images inline at their timestamps as block-level ![[image-NN.png]] elements, snapping to the nearest sentence boundary
- Multiple images in close succession (animated build-up) appear consecutively with whatever prose falls between them
- Words verbatim — soft-corrects per Phase C policy applied during reflow, logged to frontmatter

Body sections:
- ## TL;DR
- ## Key takeaways
- ## <chapter 1 title>  (verbatim from chapter_markers)
  prose with inline images
- ## <chapter 2 title>
  prose with inline images
- ...
- ## References  (filtered URLs from description)
- ## Commands & Code  (omit if empty)

Very short videos (≤ 3 min) or videos with no chapter_markers: render the entire transcript as one prose flow under '## Body' (or similar — open). No chapter sections.

See docs/youtube-wiki-design.md → 'Body structure (option D — decoupled prose flow)'.
close_reason: SKILL.md rewritten for Phase 1.5: value-filter sampling (A), frontmatter schema with source_description + transcript_corrections (B), description-driven references + hallucination check + soft-correct policy (C), chapter-level prose flow with inline images (D)

title: synthesizer Phase C: description-driven references + hallucination check + soft-correct policy
description:
Extend Phase C beyond the existing TL;DR / takeaways / tags / topics / commands extraction with three new responsibilities.

1. Description-driven references:
   - Fetch the video description via yt-dlp (info['description'])
   - Extract URLs (regex on http/https/www. prefixes; expand bare domain references)
   - Classify each URL per the keep/drop table in the design doc:
     - KEEP: author resource pages, project/tool homepages, papers/articles/books, GitHub, related videos, community spaces with specific in-video reference, standalone documentation
     - DROP: Patreon, merch/store, sponsor codes, generic social media, generic newsletters, linktree-style aggregators
   - Emit kept references as bullets with (a) the actual URL and (b) a one-line annotation noting why it was cited
   - Also emit plain-text references for people/papers named in-video without explicit URLs

2. Hallucination check via description:
   - Compare the agent-generated TL;DR against the description's 'ABOUT THIS VIDEO' prose (or full description if no clear 'about' section)
   - If TL;DR introduces framing or claims not grounded in either the transcript or description, re-do the TL;DR
   - Bias toward attribution-implicit rather than asserting claims as fact (a sources/videos/ entry is by definition 'what the video said')

3. Auto-caption soft-correct policy:
   - When walking the transcript for prose composition, identify obvious mis-transcriptions: homophones (kills↔scales, their↔there, write↔right), garbled compound words, dropped articles in otherwise grammatical sentences
   - Soft-correct only when the intended word is unambiguous from immediate context
   - Bias toward leaving alone — better one piece of garbled text than an inverted meaning
   - Log every correction in 'transcript_corrections' frontmatter

See docs/youtube-wiki-design.md → 'Description-driven references', 'Description as hallucination check', 'Auto-caption soft-correct policy'.
close_reason: SKILL.md rewritten for Phase 1.5: value-filter sampling (A), frontmatter schema with source_description + transcript_corrections (B), description-driven references + hallucination check + soft-correct policy (C), chapter-level prose flow with inline images (D)

title: synthesizer frontmatter schema: source_description + transcript_corrections + image-NN.png rename
description:
Extend the frontmatter schema with two new fields and rename the per-frame asset filename.

New frontmatter fields:
- 'source_description: |' — the verbatim author-written description from yt-dlp (info['description']). Block-scalar YAML so multi-line is fine. Used by the synthesizer Phase C as a hallucination check against the agent-generated TL;DR; persisted in frontmatter for searchable reference.
- 'transcript_corrections: []' — list of {original: 'kills', corrected: 'scales', timestamp: 320} entries logging every soft-correct the synthesizer applied per the auto-caption policy. Auditable; lets a future linter spot systematic captioner failures.

Filename rename:
- scene-NN.png → image-NN.png (the 'scene' abstraction is being dropped; image is more accurate)
- Update both the file write logic and the body's wikilink references (![[image-NN.png]])

Both base-schema-shared fields ('source_description') and source-type-specific fields ('transcript_corrections') stay strict key-value, no narrative prose. List of objects is fine — the schema discipline is 'structured data ok, narrative content goes in the body.'

See docs/youtube-wiki-design.md → Frontmatter schema.
close_reason: SKILL.md rewritten for Phase 1.5: value-filter sampling (A), frontmatter schema with source_description + transcript_corrections (B), description-driven references + hallucination check + soft-correct policy (C), chapter-level prose flow with inline images (D)

title: synthesizer Phase A: value-filter sampling (replaces scene-boundary detection)
description:
Replace the same/different/additive verdict scheme in Phase A.4 with a value-based kind classification.

Current behavior (broken):
- pHash + agent vision classify each consecutive pair as same/different/additive
- 'different' marks a scene boundary; runs of same/additive consolidate into one scene with one canonical frame
- Result: every visual change becomes a 'scene' regardless of whether the new content is informational

New behavior:
- Each candidate frame gets classified by KIND:
  - DROP: talking-head, blank/transition, title-card (chapter dividers), pull-quote-card (text duplicating transcript)
  - KEEP: diagram, map, chart, code, ui, b-roll, animated-build-step, hybrid-with-info-overlay
- pHash becomes a deduplication check (Hamming ≤ 5 between consecutive keepers → drop one) rather than a scene-boundary trigger
- Bisect the GAP between consecutive keepers when > ~15s AND the intervening transcript suggests visual content ('as you can see', 'let me show you', etc.)
- Inside an animated-build-step run: sample densely (every 2-3s) and keep ALL the steps — do not collapse to the terminal frame

Output: ordered list of (timestamp, frame_path, kind) triples for kept frames. NOT 'scenes covering the whole video' — just informational moments and where they are. Phase B (scene consolidation) is dissolved by this change.

See docs/youtube-wiki-design.md → 'The value-filter principle' for full spec.
close_reason: SKILL.md rewritten for Phase 1.5: value-filter sampling (A), frontmatter schema with source_description + transcript_corrections (B), description-driven references + hallucination check + soft-correct policy (C), chapter-level prose flow with inline images (D)

title: Phase 1.5: youtube-synthesizer rework based on Phase 1 e2e learnings
description:
The Phase 1 e2e validation (ih2.10) on the grain-haul test video (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MIqpvpNS5pI) surfaced four structural flaws in the synthesizer's image selection and output formatting:

1. Image selection is too inclusive — captured 19 'scenes' of which only ~6 were informational; the rest were talking-head shots, blank transition frames, and title/pull-quote cards.
2. Per-scene heading hierarchy + transcript-by-scene-window cuts clauses mid-sentence.
3. References emit vague prose ('the presenter's Discord and website') instead of the actual URLs sitting in the video description.
4. Auto-caption mis-transcriptions ('kills for measurement' → meant 'scales for measurement') propagate verbatim, breaking future full-text search.

This epic captures the rework. The synthesizer SKILL.md will be substantially rewritten (Phases A and D); the screenshotter is unchanged.

See docs/youtube-wiki-design.md for the full revised design (rewritten 2026-04-28). Specifically:
- 'The value-filter principle' section replaces the original sampling/bisection design
- 'Body structure (option D — decoupled prose flow)' replaces the per-scene rendering
- 'Description-driven references' adds URL fetching + filtering
- 'Auto-caption soft-correct policy' addresses the homophone-error issue
- 'Phase 1.5 work breakdown' enumerates the 5 child issues
close_reason: Phase 1.5 e2e revalidation passed: 15 load-bearing images (vs 19 noisy in Phase 1); chapter-level prose with sentence-aware reflow; no mid-sentence cuts; description-driven references (thegrainbound.com + discord.gg) with annotations; soft-correct 'kills'->'scales' applied and logged to transcript_corrections frontmatter; source_description populated; preview.py script added for headless-box rendering. Two SKILL.md tightenings landed (sampling cadence, chapter-boundary snapping). Open follow-ups for the next round are image-quality issues (user request) -- new issues will be filed against the youtube-synthesizer skill, not under t1k.

title: synthesizer: bisection + LLM judgment loop using parent agent's vision
description:
The smart core of the youtube-synthesizer skill. Replaces the relocated work from ih2.3 (vision-LLM judge) and ih2.4-original (transcript-aligned bisection pipeline).

Approach: the synthesizer is a Claude Code skill. When invoked, the parent Claude Code agent reads the SKILL.md and follows the procedure. The procedure includes opening frame images via the Read tool and reasoning over them — no separate API calls, no separate billing, just the agent's native vision capability.

Procedure:
1. Get transcript via youtube-transcript skill
2. Decide initial sample timestamps: transcript snippet boundaries + chapter starts (from yt-dlp metadata via screenshotter's --no-download metadata mode)
3. Call screenshotter extract with those timestamps; receive manifest with frames + pHashes
4. For each consecutive pair, classify by pHash band:
   - same (Hamming ≤5): drop one without further work
   - different (Hamming ≥20): keep both without further work
   - ambiguous (middle band): agent opens both image files, looks at them with the transcript span between as context, decides one of {same, different, additive}
5. For different/additive pairs where bisection might reveal more, schedule additional timestamps. Re-call screenshotter (cached frames are free to re-request)
6. Continue bisecting until terminal conditions:
   - Window <1s, OR
   - Endpoints judged 'same', OR
   - Additive build-up reaches its terminal frame (forward bisection until 'same' or 'different')
7. Emit final list of (timestamp, frame_path, transcript_span, verdict) tuples for downstream scene consolidation (ih2.6)

Bias toward false positives. Calling 'different' on actual-same wastes one frame in the wiki (cheap). Calling 'same' on actual-different loses information forever (expensive).

This issue produces:
- The SKILL.md procedure document the agent follows
- Any helper scripts the agent invokes (probably just the screenshotter extract CLI from ih2.4)

See docs/youtube-wiki-design.md (post-pivot architecture).
close_reason: Phase A of the synthesizer SKILL.md written: bisection + visual-diff judgment procedure that the parent agent follows step by step. Covers transcript+metadata fetch, initial sampling (transcript boundaries + chapters + silence anchors), pHash-based filtering with explicit thresholds and ambiguous-band visual judgment, false-positive bias guidance with transcript-context examples, bisection rules, terminal conditions, and the (timestamp, frame_path, transcript_span, verdict) tuple-list output. Phase B/C/D placeholders flagged at the bottom. Plugin scaffolding (.claude-plugin/, skills/youtube-synthesizer/) created. End-to-end validation deferred to ih2.10 — ih2.11 ships the procedure document; subsequent issues add the consolidation/extraction/writing phases on top.

title: Refactor: Deduplicate get_comment_count function
description:
## Context
Originally devon-claude-skills-jrp (Jan 2026), recreated after Dolt migration dropped the entry. From PR #1 review (comment ID: 2709909507).

gh api /repos/devonjones/devon-claude-skills/pulls/1/comments/2709909507

## Suggestion
The 'get_comment_count' function is defined in both:
- trigger-review.sh
- get-review-comments.sh

Duplicating helper functions makes maintenance harder, as changes need to be applied in multiple places.

## Proposed Solution
Move this function into a shared script, like '_jq_helpers.sh' or a new '_github_helpers.sh', and source it where needed.

## Status note
Verify against current scripts before doing — subsequent pr-review-loop PRs (#6, #8, #9) may have already addressed this.

title: Refactor: Extract pagination logic into shared helper
description:
## Context
Originally devon-claude-skills-5zw (Jan 2026), recreated after Dolt migration dropped the entry. From PR #1 review (comment ID: 2709909502).

gh api /repos/devonjones/devon-claude-skills/pulls/1/comments/2709909502

## Suggestion
The pagination logic for fetching all review threads is duplicated in several scripts:
- get-review-comments.sh
- get-agent-comments.sh
- reopen-comment.sh
- reply-to-comment.sh
- summarize-reviews.sh

While the GraphQL queries differ slightly in the fields they fetch, the pagination boilerplate is identical.

## Proposed Solution
Create a shared helper function that takes the core query fragment as an argument and handles pagination. Could be added to _jq_helpers.sh or a new _github_helpers.sh.

## Status note
Verify against current scripts before doing — subsequent pr-review-loop PRs (#6, #8, #9) may have already addressed this.

title: e2e validation on grain-haul test video (MIqpvpNS5pI)
description:
Run full youtube-synthesizer pipeline against https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MIqpvpNS5pI (Modern History TV — medieval grain-haul economics; the central thesis about 3-day economic limits shaping baronies is visually load-bearing on radius diagrams + consumption math).

Verify:
- Transcript captured cleanly
- Scene detection picks up radius diagrams and consumption charts (NOT just talking-head shots between them)
- Additive verdicts catch any incremental diagram build-up
- Frontmatter populated correctly including channel_id (stable handle for future cross-link runs)
- Output renders correctly in Obsidian (per-note folder, embedded scene-NN.png images)
- Output is faithful capture without editorializing or pre-framing

Iterate on thresholds (pHash bands, silence trigger) and prompts as needed. Use 'bd remember' to capture tuning insights for future videos.

See docs/youtube-wiki-design.md.
close_reason: E2E validation on grain-haul test video (MIqpvpNS5pI, channel 'The Grainbound', 18:39 duration) successful. Procedure: fetched transcript via youtube-transcript (477 snippets, 20.5KB), sampled 22 timestamps spread across all 4 chapters via youtube-screenshotter extract.py (cached video reused, all 22 frames extracted, pHashes computed). Classified 22 consecutive pairs: 17 'different' by pHash, 5 ambiguous → visual judgment resolved as 1 different + 4 same. Consolidated into 19 scenes (4 talking-head pairs at the end correctly merged). Generated TL;DR + 5 takeaways + references + 8 tags + 3 topics. Wrote hierarchical (chapter→scene) markdown entry to /tmp/yt-synthesizer-test-vault/sources/videos/2026-04-27-the-ox-that-s-breaking-your-fantasy-map/index.md (19 scene-NN.png siblings, 175-line index.md). Frontmatter has all required fields including channel_id (UCSeuqv9fNSUJT5VfcmslTBg) and chapter_markers; YAML parses cleanly. Output is faithful capture, no editorializing. Insights saved to beads memory: threshold validation, scene-boundary reflow gap, t=0 sampling rule, title sanitization rule. Phase 1 of the youtube-wiki epic is complete.

title: synthesizer: SKILL.md + CLI entrypoint
description:
Write SKILL.md and CLI for youtube-synthesizer.

CLI accepts video URL + --vault path. Skill orchestrates youtube-transcript and youtube-screenshotter calls, threads results through synthesizer pipeline (scene consolidation → structured extraction → output writing).

Plugin structure: plugins/youtube-synthesizer/ with .claude-plugin/ + skills/youtube-synthesizer/SKILL.md + scripts/. Register in .claude-plugin/marketplace.json. Update README.md with installation and usage.

Handle Phase 1 failure modes gracefully:
- No transcript available (no captions, foreign language) → error early with clear message
- Video unavailable (private, members-only, age-restricted, geo-blocked) → error early
- Live streams (no fixed endpoint) → refuse for now
- Very short videos (~3min) → auto-degrade to flat structure

See docs/youtube-wiki-design.md.
close_reason: Synthesizer plugin scaffolding complete. SKILL.md grew an Invocation section (URL + vault path required, --rerun and --why optional) and an Output summary at top, before the four-phase procedure. Registered in .claude-plugin/marketplace.json. README.md updated with install command, features list, usage example showing natural-language invocation, and a Prerequisites entry pointing at the companion plugins. Plugin installable via /plugin install youtube-synthesizer@devon-claude-skills. End-to-end validation on the grain-haul video is ih2.10.

title: synthesizer: output writing — folder structure, hierarchical-vs-flat, image storage
description:
Assemble all pieces and write to the target Obsidian vault.

Path: <vault>/sources/videos/<ingested-date>-<sanitized-title>/
Files: index.md (frontmatter + body) + scene-NN.png siblings (canonical frames per scene)

Body structure:
1. TL;DR
2. Key takeaways
3. Scene-by-scene narration with embedded frames
   - Hierarchical (chapter → scenes) when chapter_markers exist
   - Flat otherwise
   - Auto-degrade to flat for very short videos (~3min) regardless of chapter markers
4. References / Mentioned
5. Commands & Code (for howto content)

Skip-by-default if entry already exists at the target path. --rerun flag overwrites.

Per-note folder (vs shared attachments/) — each entry self-contains its assets, portable.

See docs/youtube-wiki-design.md for the full output spec.
close_reason: Phase D added to synthesizer SKILL.md: entry path resolution (sanitize title, sources/videos/<ingested-date>-<sanitized-title>/), skip-or-overwrite gate (--rerun flag), canonical frame copying as scene-NN.png, hierarchical-vs-flat decision (flat for ≤3min or no chapters; hierarchical otherwise with uncategorized fallback), full body structure (frontmatter + title + Source line + TL;DR + Key takeaways + Scenes + References + Commands & Code), Obsidian wikilink-style image embeds (![[scene-NN.png]]), transcript reflow allowed but no word changes, MM:SS / HH:MM:SS time formatting, post-write YAML parse-check. Footer flipped to indicate all phases covered; ih2.10 e2e validation is next.

title: synthesizer: TL;DR + takeaways + references + code + tags extraction
description:
LLM-driven structured extraction from transcript + scene structure. Produce:

- TL;DR: 1-2 sentences. The highest-leverage section for re-discovery six months later.
- Key takeaways: 3-5 bullets
- References / Mentioned: people, papers, tools, other videos cited in passing — plain text only, NO auto-linking in Phase 1 (Phase 4+ linker resolves to wikilinks once cross-source corpus exists)
- Commands & Code: for howto videos — extracted code/command blocks duplicated at the end for quick reference
- Tags: controlled list, included in frontmatter (also drives downstream search)

Output discipline: keep style source-faithful. NO editorializing, NO pre-framing for downstream synthesis directions. The vault root is where the user weaves video content into their mental model — the skill must not bias that weaving.

See docs/youtube-wiki-design.md.
close_reason: Phase C added to synthesizer SKILL.md: structured extraction sections for TL;DR (1-2 sentences, central thesis), key takeaways (3-5 bullets, substantive claims not headers), references / mentioned (plain text, no auto-wikilinks in Phase 1), commands & code (verbatim from canonical frames + transcript narration; omitted for non-technical videos), and tags+topics for frontmatter (kebab-case, controlled list, no vague entries). Source-faithful discipline emphasized in each section. Footer updated.

title: synthesizer: scene consolidation + frontmatter generation
description:
First piece of the youtube-synthesizer skill, post-bisection. Take the output of the synthesizer's bisection + judgment loop (list of (timestamp, frame_path, transcript_span, verdict) tuples), consolidate runs of 'same'/'additive' into single scenes, attach all transcript spans that occurred during each scene to its canonical frame.

Generate frontmatter from yt-dlp metadata (already in the screenshotter's manifest). Schema (strict key-value, no nested prose):

Base (shared with future article/pdf/substack source-skills):
- source_type: youtube
- source_url, source_title, source_author
- source_published_date, ingested_date
- tags, topics, why_ingested

YouTube-specific extras:
- video_id, channel, channel_id (stable handle, survives renames)
- playlist_id, playlist_title (null unless invoked via playlist URL)
- duration_seconds, chapter_markers

Frontmatter is the contract for the future sqlite3 indexer — keep it machine-parseable, no narrative content.

See docs/youtube-wiki-design.md.
close_reason: Phase B added to synthesizer SKILL.md: scene consolidation algorithm (walk the verdict list, runs of same/additive collapse into scenes with the additive-terminal-frame becoming canonical, different boundaries close scenes) and the frontmatter schema (base shared-with-future-source-skills + YouTube-specific extras, all populated from the screenshotter's metadata block). Frontmatter discipline noted (strict key-value, no nested prose). Tags/topics/why_ingested/body content explicitly deferred to Phase C/D. Footer updated.

title: screenshotter: SKILL.md + CLI entrypoint
description:
Write SKILL.md and CLI for youtube-screenshotter following the convention used by youtube-transcript and other skills in this repo.

CLI accepts video URL + output directory + optional thresholds (pHash bands, silence trigger). Writes frames + a manifest JSON with the (timestamp, frame_path, transcript_span, verdict) tuples.

Plugin structure: plugins/youtube-screenshotter/ with .claude-plugin/ + skills/youtube-screenshotter/SKILL.md + scripts/. Register in .claude-plugin/marketplace.json. Update README.md with installation instructions and usage examples.

This skill is generic — it does not know about wikis or Obsidian. Output is reusable for future tools (video summarization, scene extraction, etc.).

See docs/youtube-wiki-design.md.
close_reason: Plugin scaffolding complete: SKILL.md describes the screenshotter as mechanical-only (no LLM, no scene detection); documents extract.py + the three sub-scripts; documents pHash bands and points at youtube-synthesizer for the smart layer. Registered in .claude-plugin/marketplace.json. Updated README.md with install command, features, usage examples, and prerequisites entry. Plugin is now installable via /plugin install youtube-screenshotter@devon-claude-skills.

title: screenshotter: unified extract CLI batching download + frames + pHash
description:
With the LLM judge relocated to the synthesizer (see ih2.3 close note), this issue becomes a thinner integration: expose a single 'extract' command that takes a video URL + a list of timestamps, calls the existing primitives (video.py for download/metadata, frames.py for extraction, phash.py for hashes), and emits one manifest JSON.

Manifest shape:
- video metadata block (video_id, source_url, duration, channel_id, chapter_markers, etc — once per manifest)
- entries: list of (timestamp, frame_path, phash) per requested timestamp

This is the stable interface the synthesizer's bisection loop will call — possibly multiple times during a single video processing run (initial sample + refined timestamps after judgments). The screenshotter caches frames by filename, so re-extraction at known timestamps is free.

No LLM calls in the screenshotter. No bisection logic — that lives in the synthesizer.

See docs/youtube-wiki-design.md (post-pivot architecture).
close_reason: extract.py composes video.py + frames.py + phash.py into a single CLI emitting one manifest JSON per call (video_path, full metadata, entries[{timestamp, frame_path, phash}]). Auto-detects cached video file by matching <video_id>.{mp4,mkv,webm} in output_dir; if present, skips download and just runs metadata probe. Frame cache from ih2.2 still applies, so re-requesting a known timestamp is free. Verified on grain-haul video at /tmp/yt-screenshotter-test: cached video + 2 cached frames + 1 fresh extraction completed in 2.4s. Manifest shape matches the issue spec. Sibling imports work via sys.path.insert(0, Path(__file__).parent) at the top of the script.

title: screenshotter: vision-LLM diff judge with transcript context
description:
Vision-LLM judge for ambiguous frame pairs from the pHash pre-filter. Use Claude Haiku 4.5 with two image inputs and the transcript span between the two timestamps as context.

Verdict is exactly one of:
- same: no meaningful change
- different: new content / scene change
- additive: frame B contains everything in A plus more (slide animations, code typing, equation reveals)

Bias prompt toward false positives — when uncertain, prefer different/additive. Calling 'different' on actual-same wastes one frame in the wiki (cheap). Calling 'same' on actual-different loses information forever (expensive).

Use prompt caching on the system prompt and judge schema. Per-call input is two images + a transcript snippet, so caching covers the system prompt only.

See docs/youtube-wiki-design.md for the full diff-judge design.
close_reason: Architectural pivot: vision-LLM judging moves out of the screenshotter (where it would need a separate ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and double-bill against Claude Code's existing inference) and into the synthesizer's parent-agent reasoning (which natively has vision and runs on the user's existing Claude Code session). The screenshotter becomes mechanical-only (download + frames + pHash). The bisection + judge loop becomes synthesizer orchestration logic. New issue created downstream for the relocated work. Script (judge.py) deleted from working tree before commit.

title: screenshotter: ffmpeg frame extraction at timestamps + pHash pre-filter
description:
Given a downloaded video and a list of timestamps, extract frames as PNGs via ffmpeg. Implement perceptual-hash diffing (pHash via imagehash or equivalent) for cheap pre-filtering.

Define three bands via Hamming distance thresholds: very-similar → declare 'same' without LLM call; very-different → declare 'different' without LLM call; ambiguous middle band → defer to vision LLM judge (separate issue). Thresholds tunable via constants. Expect 60-80% of frame pairs decided by pHash alone in practice.

See docs/youtube-wiki-design.md for sampling design.
close_reason: Two scripts: frames.py extracts video frames at given timestamps via ffmpeg (filename-based cache so re-extraction is ~free); phash.py provides three-band classifier (same/different/ambiguous) on 64-bit pHash Hamming distance with default thresholds SAME_MAX=5, DIFFERENT_MIN=20 (tunable kwargs). Verified on grain-haul video: 7 frames extracted, 7 pair comparisons exhibit the full verdict spectrum, cached re-extraction is ~100ms for 3 frames. Imports: 'from frames import extract_frames'; 'from phash import compute, classify_pair'.

title: screenshotter: yt-dlp video download + metadata extraction
description:
Foundation layer for youtube-screenshotter skill. Download video to a working directory and capture all metadata fields needed for downstream synthesis: channel name, channel_id (stable across renames), title, source_published_date, duration_seconds, chapter_markers, playlist_id, playlist_title.

Use yt-dlp's --dump-json to extract metadata in one pass. Target 720p resolution to balance quality vs. download time and disk usage. Cleanup behavior: keep video file for the duration of one synthesizer run (frames are extracted from it), delete after.

See docs/youtube-wiki-design.md for context.
close_reason: Foundation script video.py downloads at 720p and emits all required metadata (channel, channel_id, title, source_published_date, duration_seconds, chapter_markers, playlist_id, playlist_title). Verified end-to-end against MIqpvpNS5pI: 56MB MP4 at 1280x720, all 8 metadata fields populated, 4 chapter markers detected, 5s wall time. --no-download flag also added for cheap metadata-only iteration. Importable from siblings as 'from video import download'.

title: youtube-wiki: extract searchable knowledge from YouTube videos into Obsidian vaults
description:
Three-skill pipeline turning YouTube videos (explainers, howtos, lectures) into Obsidian literature notes for a personal searchable corpus. Produces faithful capture (transcript + canonical visual scenes + structured extraction), leaving the user's synthesis layer free to weave content downstream.

See docs/youtube-wiki-design.md for the full architectural design, frontmatter schema, sampling strategy, and phasing.

Phase 1 (this epic): transcript + new screenshotter + new synthesizer producing in-place wiki entries with embedded video frames. Test target: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MIqpvpNS5pI (grain-haul economics, visuals load-bearing).

Future phases captured in design doc:
- Phase 2: nano-banana imagery regen for publishable / copyright-safe output
- Phase 3: youtube-comments skill + integration
- Phase 4+: sqlite3 corpus indexer, cross-source linker, parallel source-skills (articles, pdfs, substack)
close_reason: All 11 child issues closed. Phase 1 of the youtube-wiki epic is complete: youtube-transcript (already shipped), youtube-screenshotter (mechanical primitives + extract CLI + plugin scaffold + marketplace registration), and youtube-synthesizer (4-phase SKILL.md procedure: bisection+visual-diff judgment, scene consolidation+frontmatter, structured extraction, output writing) all live on main. End-to-end validation against the grain-haul test video produced a clean hierarchical Obsidian entry (see ih2.10 close note). Architectural pivot during build: vision-LLM judging moved from a Python subprocess (which would need a separate ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) to the synthesizer's parent-agent reasoning (which uses the user's existing Claude Code session — no double-billing). Phase 2 (nano-banana imagery regen for publishable output), Phase 3 (youtube-comments), and Phase 4+ (sqlite3 corpus indexer + cross-source linker + parallel source-skills) remain in docs/youtube-wiki-design.md as future work.

title: Add youtube-transcript skill
description: Port the youtube-transcript skill pattern from intellectronica/agent-skills into our marketplace: Python via uv run --script, youtube-transcript-api dependency, plain/timestamped output, write-to-file support. Adds plugins/youtube-transcript/ and registers in marketplace.json.
close_reason: Opened PR #10

title: Port pr-review-loop improvements from Valid-Eval fork
description: Port selected features/fixes from Valid-Eval/valid-eval-skills pr-review-loop: Gemini already-reviewed skip, get-pr-comments.sh, SKILL.md guidance (self-contradiction detection, hard round ceiling, merge readiness, TODO-beads fallback, AGENT-REVIEWERS suggestion), and AGENT-REVIEWERS.md additions (shell-script-reviewer, generic guidelines). Plan at ~/.claude/plans/stateless-sniffing-curry.md.
close_reason: Ported and opened PR #9

title: Back-port discover-agents.sh from valid-eval-skills PR #12
description: Port the deterministic AGENT-REVIEWERS.md discovery script (scripts/discover-agents.sh) from Valid-Eval/valid-eval-skills#12 and update SKILL.md to reference it. Replaces vague prose-based discovery with a testable script.
close_reason: Closed

value: youtube-synthesizer e2e: scene transcript boundaries cut mid-sentence are a real readability issue when sample timestamps don't align with sentence ends. The SKILL.md says reflow is allowed within a scene's transcript span; implementations should split paragraph breaks at sentence boundaries (period+space) before composing the scene body, not just dump the raw snippet concatenation. Without reflow, scene N's transcript can end like 'An ox eats roughly 10% of' with scene N+1 starting 'its cargo every 50 miles'.

youtube-synthesizer pHash dedup threshold (≤5 hamming) is too strict for visually-redundant frames. Phase 1.5 e2e against MIqpvpNS5pI: two opening map frames at t=0.0 and t=0.5 measured hamming=28 (different) despite being effectively the same fantasy map (slight pan/zoom). Both kept by strict rule. Visual judgment would dedup. Consider: agent-judged dedup pass after pHash, or a higher threshold (~15) for same-kind consecutive keepers. Tracked for future tuning.

Image-read request size limit: reading too many full-res PNG frames in one tool call hits 'Request too large (max 32MB)'. Frames at 1280x720 PNG are ~500KB-1.5MB each; ~20 reads in a single message can blow the limit. Always batch image reads in groups of 2-3 when classifying or auditing the synthesizer's keep-list. Per-second thumbnails at 320x180 JPEG would be much smaller (~10-30KB each) but currently used only by discover.py internally.

youtube-synthesizer initial-sampling rule: ALWAYS include t=0.5 in the timestamp list. Phase A.2 lists this explicitly but the e2e test missed it (started at t=5) and lost the first ~5s of intro transcript. The video start frame is also commonly distinct (logo, title card) and should not be skipped. The transcript-boundary heuristic alone won't catch this — most transcripts start at t=0.04 or similar, which rounds away.

youtube-synthesizer Phase 1.5 e2e against MIqpvpNS5pI: produced 15 informational images (vs Phase 1's 19, vs t1k.5's '~6' estimate). The '~6' was a low estimate; each lord type in this video has 2-3 distinct illustrations (chokepoint: king-in-pass + castle-in-pass; surplus: farmer + caloric-battery + boat; junction: scales+barn + lord+map; specialist: NO-RIVER+cart + ADDS-VALUE) and the value-filter rule keeps all of them since each communicates something distinct. Plus 3 maps (full world + zoomed detail + region-with-red-dot) and 1 opener map. So 'expected count' depends on video; only floor is 'load-bearing only.'

youtube-synthesizer pHash thresholds (SAME_MAX=5, DIFFERENT_MIN=20) validated against the grain-haul test video (MIqpvpNS5pI): coarse sampling at 30-100s intervals produced 22 consecutive pairs, of which 17 were unambiguously 'different' by pHash and 5 fell in the ambiguous band. Visual judgment of the 5: 1 was different (CHAPTER 3 title card → THREE DAY RULE subsection card), 4 were same (sustained talking-head shots with only facial-expression changes). Conclusion: thresholds are right; ambiguous-pair count scales with sample density and amount of static talking-head content.

youtube-synthesizer Phase A rewrite — value-filter principle. Images appear in the wiki entry only when they communicate something the transcript words don't, OR when they help explain the narrative (even if multiple in a row). The 'scene' abstraction dissolves: no per-scene heading, no canonical-frame-per-scene. Instead: classify every candidate frame by KIND. Drop talking-head, blank/transition, pull-quote-text-cards, chapter-divider cards. Keep diagram, map, chart, code, ui, b-roll, animated-build-steps (all of them, not just the terminal), and hybrid-talking-head-with-info-overlay. Bisection trigger flips: bisect the GAP between consecutive keep-frames when it exceeds N seconds, looking for missed diagrams in talking-head-dominated stretches. Test target MIqpvpNS5pI ('The Ox That's Breaking Your Fantasy Map') was deliberately chosen for this failure mode — ~70% talking-head + visual-interest filler, ~30% load-bearing diagrams. Current implementation captured 19 scenes; correct implementation should produce ~6 informational images. Pairs with option D (decoupled prose body, images inline at timestamps).

youtube-synthesizer Phase D rewrite: decouple narrative from visual scenes (option D from the post-e2e flaw discussion). Body becomes one continuous chapter-level prose flow with sentence-aware reflow over the full chapter span; images drop inline as block elements at their canonical timestamps without breaking the prose. The 'Scene 1, Scene 2…' heading hierarchy goes away — scenes become anonymous image anchors. Motivated by the e2e output where scene boundaries cut clauses mid-sentence (e.g. 'use the map and work' / 'along. I will not show…' split across two scene blocks). Tracking implementation as a Phase D rewrite; current SKILL.md Phase D is the to-be-replaced version.

youtube-synthesizer title sanitization: SKILL.md Phase D.1 says 'strip anything that isn't [a-z0-9-]' (apostrophes/quotes disappear cleanly, leaving 'thats' from 'that's'), NOT replace-with-hyphen (which produces 'that-s' — visually awkward). The e2e build script used the wrong rule and produced '...that-s-breaking...' as the folder name. SKILL.md spec is correct; future implementations should follow the strip-don't-replace rule.

youtube-synthesizer Phase A classification drift: when classifying 100+ candidate frames in batches of 10-15, per-frame attention drift causes some talking-head and transition frames to get mistakenly tagged as keep-kinds. Phase 1.6 e2e against MIqpvpNS5pI: 4 talking-head frames + 1 empty cartoon + 1 redundant build-up state slipped through (6/33 = 18% error rate). Mitigation: add a Phase A.7 whole-list audit pass that re-reads every kept frame as a unit and prunes the noise. The audit is a separate pass from A.4 classification; its job is to catch drift and curate.
